marketState: neutral — extreme short positioning and incoming heat wave offset elevated storage and bearish injection
summary: Henry Hub front-month at $3.259 sits $0.30 above the fair-value midpoint of $2.958 but comfortably within the $2.46–$3.46 band, leaving no urgent valuation call in either direction. The dominant near-term tension is a split weather picture: days 1-7 demand runs 7.4% below the 5-year average (bearish), while days 8-16 is forecasted above the 5-year maximum (+22.5%, rangePos 1.19, aboveMax=true), which is an historically rare demand spike that should tighten the supply-demand balance materially once it rolls in. Storage at +153 Bcf above the 5-year average with a fresh bearish injection surprise (+25 Bcf vs the +48 Bcf consensus) is the chief structural headwind. The most striking data point is managed-money net positioning at the 3rd percentile over 5 years (-122,617 contracts net short), meaning speculative shorts are at a 5-year extreme and represent enormous latent short-covering fuel if the incoming heat materialises; shorter timeframes (5m/15m RSI below 30, price below lower Bollinger) are already flagging an oversold condition consistent with a near-term mean-reversion bounce.

longTerm.rationale: Storage is +153 Bcf above the 5-year average and the most recent injection was a 25 Bcf bearish surprise, making a sustained multi-week rally difficult without a material storage draw narrative. The weekly chart shows a bearish 50<200 EMA cross, and the 4-week probability of a higher price is only 31% with a typical 4-week move of -2.3% — the seasonal setup does not support an aggressive directional position at this horizon. While extreme COT short positioning (3rd percentile) is a contrarian signal, it is not yet confirmed by a catalytic price break, and low seasonal reliability (44pp spread) compounds the uncertainty.
longTerm.trigger: Enter long when storage deviates narrow to within ±50 Bcf of the 5-year average AND/OR price posts a weekly close above $3.47 (upper edge of the current fair-value band). Enter short if the incoming heat wave fails to materialise and price breaks below $2.95 (fair-value midpoint) on above-average volume.

shortTerm.stop: $3.13 (below recent structural support; ~$0.13 risk from mid-entry $3.265)
shortTerm.rationale: Short-timeframe technicals (5m and 15m RSI below 30, price below lower Bollinger on both) set up a textbook oversold mean-reversion bounce, corroborated by a bullish trend and positive MACD histogram on the 4h chart. The decisive catalyst is the days 8-16 weather forecast: +22.5% above the 5-year average and above the historical maximum, meaning the market should begin pricing in a meaningful demand shock within the 1-10 day window. With managed-money net short at the 3rd percentile in 5 years, even moderate bullish newsflow risks a sharp short-covering rally.
shortTerm.invalidation: Daily close below $3.13, or a next storage report showing an injection above +90 Bcf (widening the 5-year surplus further).
